topics.summary: Official campaigning for the June 3 nationwide local elections began on May 21 with over 7,800 candidates. The DPK holds leads in most major races but the PPP has narrowed the gap significantly, with the Seoul mayoral race tightening to a single-digit spread (DPK's Chong Won-o at 40% vs PPP's Oh Se-hoon at 37%).

evidence_headlines.title: Campaign officially begins for June 3 elections with over 7,800 candidates
Seoul Race Tightens, Busan Neck-and-Neck, Independent Surges in Jeonbuk
Official campaigning for June 3 local elections begins

topics.topic_en: Yoon Insurrection Trial Appeal Verdict Imminent
topics.summary: The appeals court verdict in Yoon Suk-yeol's insurrection trial is scheduled for May 28, just six days away, drawing intense media focus ahead of local elections. Yoon was sentenced to life imprisonment at first instance in February 2026; the PPP has officially stated it will issue no leadership position on the verdict.

evidence_headlines.title: Yoon Denounces Insurrection Verdict as PPP Clings to Ousted Leader
무기징역에도 반성 없는 尹‥심판은 진행 중
윤석열 내란 재판/전개

topics.topic_en: Lee Jae-myung Approval Rating One-Year Mark
topics.summary: A Gallup Korea poll released May 22 puts President Lee Jae-myung's approval at 64%, up 3 points from the prior week, with DPK party support at 46% versus PPP's 21%. The upcoming local elections are widely framed as the first national verdict on the Lee government's first year in office.

evidence_headlines.title: 이재명 대통령 취임 1년 지지율 64%...역대 2위 [한국갤럽]
Lee's Approval Rating Hits 61%, Democratic Party at 45%, People Power 23%
[MBC여론조사] 이 대통령 지지율, 서울·부산·대구 60% 넘겨

topics.topic_en: Fabricated Prosecution Special Counsel Bill Controversy
topics.summary: The DPK is pushing a 'fabricated prosecution special counsel' bill through the National Assembly in May that includes a power to withdraw charges from cases initiated under the Yoon administration, potentially including charges against President Lee himself. The PPP calls it a self-pardon bill and has vowed to block it; even some minor progressive parties have expressed opposition.

evidence_headlines.title: Opposition Slams Prosecution Special Counsel Bill; Ruling Party Weighs Revisions
[EXPLAINER] Special counsel bill sparks debate over power to drop ongoing trials
민주당 '조작기소 바로잡을 특검 필요' vs 국민의힘 '셀프 면죄부'

topics.topic_en: Constitutional Amendment Bill Blocked by PPP Boycott
topics.summary: A six-party constitutional amendment bill that would have required National Assembly approval before martial law could be imposed was blocked in May 7–8 National Assembly votes after the PPP boycotted the sessions. The proposal, backed by the DPK and five other parties, had aimed for a simultaneous referendum with the June 3 local elections.

evidence_headlines.title: Korea's First Constitutional Amendment Bid in 39 Years Collapses Amid Partisan Clash
Constitutional Amendment Fails as Opposition Boycotts Vote
개헌안 표결 '디데이'…'국힘 반대 당론'에 처리 불투명

topics.topic_en: Busan Buk-A By-Election Conservative Vote Split
topics.topic_ko: 부산 북갑 보선 보수 단일화 갈등
topics.summary: The Busan Buk-A parliamentary by-election, running alongside the local elections, has become a flashpoint for PPP internal divisions: former PPP leader Han Dong-hoon is running as an independent against PPP candidate Park Min-sik, splitting the conservative vote. Polls show DPK's Ha Jung-woo leading with Han and Park in close competition behind him; Park refused any candidacy unification on May 22.

evidence_headlines.title: 부산 북갑 기호 나왔다…하정우 1번, 박민식 2번, 한동훈 6번
박민식 '단일화 없다…부산 북구 주민에 대한 배신 행위'
[MBC여론조사] 부산 북구갑 하정우 38% 한동훈 33%

topics.summary: The PPP and minor parties have intensified criticism of the Lee government's US tariff deal, arguing it constitutes a treaty requiring National Assembly ratification that the executive has bypassed. With Korea's effective tariff rate still elevated at around 12%, the opposition frames the deal as legally and economically deficient.

evidence_headlines.title: 미국과의 관세협상을 타결했습니다
2025년 세계 무역 전쟁/대한민국
Q1 2026 U.S.-Korea Trade, Investment, and Diplomacy Ledger

topics.topic_en: High Oil Prices Economy Supplementary Budget
topics.summary: Prolonged Middle East conflict has kept oil prices elevated, prompting the Lee government to pass a first supplementary budget in April 2026 providing energy subsidies to lower-income households. KDI's May economic outlook notes a continued recovery trend but flagged high-oil-price inflation as a significant downside risk.

evidence_headlines.title: 2026년 5월 최근 경제동향
고유가 피해지원금
국민 61% '지방선거 동시 개헌 국민투표 찬성'

method_notes: Captured via Anthropic API (Sonnet 4.6) with web_search for live Korean political coverage. Topic labels (topic_en) are stable handles for cross-snapshot rank comparison.
